zh.lifeSummary: 當Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed於大約1256年出生在威爾士丹比爾利安法爾 代福瑞 可爾德時，他的父親Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig28歲，母親Nest ferch Tudur22歲。他和Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed至少有2個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed于大约1256年出生在威尔士丹比爾利安法爾 代福瑞 可爾德时，他的父亲Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig28岁，母亲Nest ferch Tudur22岁。他和Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed至少有2个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: Madog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name, possibly having the sense ‘fortunate’ or alternatively from Celtic aodh ‘fire’.

nameMeanings.name: "Edward"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed was born about 1256, in Llanfair Dyffryn Clwyd, Denbighshire, Wales, his father, Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig, was 28 and his mother, Nest ferch Tudur, was 22. He had at least 2 sons with Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed.

fr.of Sutton,Is-y-coed,Denbighshire,Wales: Sutton, Denbighshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed est né environ 1256, à Llanfair Dyffryn Clwyd, Denbighshire, Galles, son père, Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Nest ferch Tudur, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed.

de.lifeSummary: Als Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed um 1256 in Llanfair Dyffryn Clwyd, Denbighshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig 28 und seine Mutter Nest ferch Tudur 22. Er hatte mit Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed mindestens 2 Söhne.

ko.lifeSummary: Madog "Edward"  ap Ednyfed(이)가 대략 1256년 웨일스 덴비그h실르 랜파어 디프린 클w이드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ednyfed "Gôch" ap Cynwrig(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Nest ferch Tudur(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Mrs. Madog Edward ap Ednyfed(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.
